title: [main.c:159-167] LED blink loop shares pattern with fatal error loop
description:
Gap found: main.c LED patterns not reviewed.

Why this was missed: LED diagnostic code considered non-critical.

The actual bug: In main(), USB failure causes 3 rapid blinks (lines 159-167) and then continues. LoRa failure causes continuous 200ms blinks forever (lines 192-197). Both use the same led_set() function and similar patterns, but USB failure is non-fatal while LoRa failure is fatal.

The subtle issue: if someone observing the device sees 3 rapid blinks, they might think something is wrong and reset. But the device would continue. The LED patterns could be more distinct:
- USB failure: 3 slow blinks (500ms), or different count
- LoRa failure: continuous fast blinks (current 200ms)

This is a UX design question, not a bug. The current code is correct. Priority 4 - suggestion only.
close_reason: Fixed: Enhanced inline comments to document the distinct LED patterns - USB failure uses 3 rapid 100ms blinks then stops, LoRa failure uses continuous 200ms blink forever. The patterns were already distinct; improved documentation to make this clearer.
